title: anil terminalis
intro: Anil terminalis, also known as Ilam sandalwood, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the Ilam district in Nepal. This plant is known for its fragrant and woody properties, similar to sandalwood, and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Anil terminalis include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its various health benefits such as reducing stress, improving sleep, and aiding in wound healing. Anil terminalis can be prepared into herbal teas, essential oils, and powders, which can be used in aromatherapy, skincare, and traditional medicine.
